byte-preserving transfer

AFTER execve, /proc/self/fd/N shows a phantom guest fd the peer /proc/<pid>/fd view (live host inspection) omits, and a dup'd-then-closed fd's view is re-resolved by inode to a still-open sibling (set/reuse). Root: under the ABI4 sentry/worker split a typed close retires proc_fdvis but not the per-process g_fdpath tracked path, and fork replays snapshot views the parent later closed. Partial fixes explored (typed-close g_fdpath clear fixes set/private/same; fork-replay liveness) but the residual reuse needs a unified guest-fd-identity teardown; clearing g_fdpath broadly regresses proc-self-exe realpath. Architectural, deferred pending vfd fd-identity rework
fork replay remains transactional with many live children
